reasoning: The FRED tool indicates a downward trend in the 1-year real interest rate over the past year (slope of -0.0035 per day), with high month-to-month volatility (recent values ranging from 0.83 to 1.72). The last value (August 2025) is 1.52, which is somewhat above the recent 6-month average (~1.25), suggesting potential mean reversion downwards in the near term. Furthermore, anticipated Fed rate cuts in late 2025 would likely put downward pressure on nominal rates, and assuming inflation expectations adjust more slowly or remain stable, real rates should continue to trend lower. Therefore, the probability that the rate will increase relative to the August baseline is below 0.5. For the September resolution, the estimate strongly leans toward a decrease (0.40). For later dates (November 2025 and February 2026), uncertainty naturally increases over the longer horizon, so estimates revert closer to 0.5 (0.45 and 0.48 respectively).
